description: Leaving provider or module versions unconstrained so a new upstream release silently changes resource defaults or schema between runs.
body: Failure mode: provider/module versions are unpinned and the lock file is absent or ignored, so a fresh init pulls a newer provider whose changed defaults produce surprise diffs or destroys on the next plan. Detection signals: a plan suddenly wants to change resources nobody edited; CI and local resolve different provider versions; behavior changed only after terraform init. Do not run shared infrastructure with unconstrained versions. Safer fallback: set explicit version constraints, commit .terraform.lock.hcl, upgrade only via deliberate init -upgrade with a reviewed plan, and verify identical versions across environments.
